title: App Stylesheet (App.css)
content:
This file contains the global CSS styles for the DocMed application. It defines the color palette, typography, layout, and styling for various components like the sidebar, navigation, main content area, and markdown rendering.

## Purpose

- Establishes a consistent visual theme across the application.
- Defines the core layout structure using Flexbox.
- Styles interactive elements, such as buttons and input fields.
- Provides specific styling for markdown content to ensure readability.
- Includes styles for loading states and empty states.

## Key Styles and Variables

### CSS Variables (Root)

- `--bg`: Background color.
- `--surface`: Primary surface color.
- `--surface-2`: Secondary surface color.
- `--border`: Primary border color.
- `--border-2`: Secondary border color.
- `--text`: Primary text color.
- `--text-2`: Secondary text color.
- `--text-3`: Tertiary text color.
- `--accent`: Accent color for highlights and interactive elements.
- `--accent-dim`: Dimmed accent color.
- `--sidebar-w`: Width of the sidebar.
- `--radius`: Border radius for elements.

### Layout

- `.layout`: Uses Flexbox to create a two-column layout (sidebar and main content).

### Sidebar (`.sidebar`)

- Defines the appearance and behavior of the collapsible sidebar.
- Includes styles for the header (`.sidebar-top`), repository title (`.repo-title`), and collapse button (`.collapse-btn`).
- The body of the sidebar (`.sidebar-body`) handles navigation elements.

### Search (`.search-wrap`, `.search-input`, etc.)

- Styles for the search input field within the sidebar, including icons and clear button.

### Navigation (`.nav`, `.nav-item`, etc.)

- Styles for the navigation links within the sidebar, including active states, badges, section labels, directories, and file items.

### Main Content (`.main`, `.page`)

- Styles the main content area, setting a maximum width and centering the content.
- Defines styles for page titles (`.page-title`) and subtitles (`.page-subtitle`).

### Statistics (`.stats-row`, `.stat-card`)

- Styles for displaying key statistics in cards, including value (`.stat-value`) and label (`.stat-label`).

### Home Sections (`.section-title`, `.file-grid`, `.file-card`)

- Styles for section titles and the grid layout for file cards on the home page.

### Commits (`.commit-list`, `.commit-item`)

- Styles for the list of commits, including individual commit items with SHA (`.commit-sha`), message (`.commit-msg`), and metadata (`.commit-meta`).

### Doc Page (`.doc-filepath`)

- Styles for the file path displayed on documentation pages.

### Markdown Rendering (`.markdown-body`)

- Comprehensive styling for common Markdown elements like headings, paragraphs, links, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, and tables, ensuring they render well within the application's theme.

### Misc (`.empty-state`, `.loading-pulse`)

- Styles for empty states and a loading pulse animation.

## Usage

This stylesheet is imported globally and its styles are applied directly to corresponding HTML elements within the React components. Developers can reference these class names in their JSX to apply the defined styling.

For example, to style a button with the primary background and text color:

```jsx
<button className="btn-primary">Click Me</button>
```

(Note: `btn-primary` is an illustrative example, actual class names would be present in the CSS).

Developers can also extend these styles by creating new CSS classes or by modifying existing ones, keeping the CSS variable system in mind for consistent theming.
